title: Keep load output serializable and universal-safe
description: A SvelteKit load function's return must serialize across the SSR boundary and must not touch browser-only APIs.
body: Use when writing or editing a +page.js/+page.server.js load. Do not return class instances, functions, or browser objects from a universal load, and do not call window/document/localStorage there. Actions: return plain serializable data, use the load's injected fetch for relayed requests, and move browser-only work into onMount or a client-only load path. Validation signals include the page rendering server-side with the data present in the initial HTML and no serialization error in the dev console. Risk boundary: a browser API in a universal load crashes SSR; secret-bearing work belongs only in +page.server.js, never in a universal load that ships to the client.
